arguments.newString:
</Sidebar.Inset>
</Sidebar.Provider>

<style>
  /* Drag toolbar */
  .bom-toolbar {
    display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 10px; align-items: center;
    border: 1px solid hsl(var(--border));
    background: hsl(var(--card));
    border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 14px;
  }
  .bom-tb-info { flex: 1; min-width: 280px; font-size: 13px; color: hsl(var(--foreground)); }
  .bom-tb-info strong { color: oklch(0.6 0.18 260); }
  .bom-grip-inline {
    font-weight: 800; color: oklch(0.6 0.18 260);
    background: oklch(0.6 0.18 260 / 0.1);
    padding: 1px 6px; border-radius: 3px; font-family: ui-monospace, monospace;
  }
  .bom-tb-actions { display: flex; gap: 6px; }

  /* Grid host */
  .bom-grid-host { position: relative; }

  /* Handle column */
  :global(.bom-hcell) {
    display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px;
  }
  :global(.bom-cbx) { width: 13px; height: 13px; accent-color: oklch(0.6 0.18 260); cursor: pointer; }
  :global(.bom-grip) {
    cursor: grab; user-select: none;
    color: hsl(var(--muted-foreground));
    font-family: ui-monospace, monospace;
    font-weight: 800; font-size: 14px;
    padding: 0 4px;
  }
  :global(.bom-grip:hover) { color: oklch(0.6 0.18 260); }
  :global(.bom-grip:active) { cursor: grabbing; }

  /* Drop indicator line */
  .bom-drop-line {
    position: absolute; height: 3px;
    background: linear-gradient(90deg, oklch(0.6 0.18 260), oklch(0.55 0.2 290));
    border-radius: 2px;
    box-shadow: 0 0 8px oklch(0.6 0.18 260 / 0.7);
    pointer-events: none;
    z-index: 50;
  }
</style>

data.content:
Funciona perfectamente. La implementación está completa:

- **Columna handle**: checkbox de selección + grip `⋮⋮` draggable en cada fila
- **Toolbar**: instrucciones de uso, botones ↑ Subir / ↓ Bajar, y Limpiar selección
- **Drag & drop**: arrastra el grip para reordenar filas entre hermanos del mismo padre (respeta la jerarquía del árbol BOM)
- **Multi-selección**: selecciona varias filas con los checkboxes y arrastra el grip de cualquiera para moverlas como bloque
- **Drop-line**: línea indicadora de posición durante el drag
- **Auto-scroll**: desplazamiento automático al acercarse a los bordes del contenedor
despues de hacer drag y reordenar, un instante despues se seleccionar varias celdas de la coluna de seleccion. porque? eso no esta correcto?

El problema es que al iniciar el drag desde el grip, el `pointerdown` burbujea hasta el `<td>` de SvGrid, que lo interpreta como inicio de selección de celdas. Cuando termina el drag y se dispara `pointerup`, SvGrid completa el rango seleccionando las celdas intermedias.

La corrección: detener `pointerdown` en el grip para que SvGrid nunca vea el inicio de la selección.

toolRequests.name: replace_string_in_file

data.reasoningText:
The user is saying that after doing a drag and reorder, a moment later several cells in the selection column get selected. This is likely because the `dragend` event fires, and then a `click` or `mouseup` event propagates to the grid's cell selection system, causing SvGrid to select cells.

The issue is that when drag ends, the browser fires a `mouseup` or `click` event on the element where the drag ended, which then gets picked up by SvGrid's cell selection mechanism.

Looking at the reference demo, they have `enableCellSelection={false}` on the SvGrid, but our BOM demo has `enableCellSelection={true}`.


We can't just disable cell selection since we need it for inline editing in the BOM. The real problem is that after a drag completes, the `mouseup` event gets interpreted as a cell selection action. I need to prevent the grid from selecting cells immediately after a drag operation finishes.

Looking at how this works: the grip element has `draggable="true"` with `ondragstart` and `ondragend` handlers, and after the drag ends, the browser fires a synthetic click event on the drag source. Since SvGrid likely has a click handler on the cells, that's what's triggering the unwanted selection.

The cleanest approach is to set a temporary flag after `dragend` that suppresses cell selection for a brief moment, or to prevent the click event from propagating to the grid's selection logic. I'm thinking about implementing this in the `HandleDragCell` function...

Actually, a better solution is to add a one-time event listener on the capture phase that stops propagation of the phantom click that fires after dragend.

Setting up event listeners to suppress... Stopping propagation on the grip element...

Actually, I need to think through how draggable elements work in browsers. When you have `draggable="true"`, the drag sequence starts with `mousedown`, then if the user moves the pointer, `dragstart` fires and subsequent mouse events get suppressed. The `dragend` event fires when the drag completes, and sometimes a synthetic `click` fires if the pointer barely moved. So stopping the `pointerdown` should prevent SvGrid's cell selection from triggering while still allowing the drag to work normally. Stopping propagation on pointer events...

I need to make sure this doesn't break the drag behavior though. `stopPropagation()` only prevents bubbling to parents, so the browser's drag API should still work on the element itself. The checkbox already has this pattern with `onclick`, so adding it to the grip should prevent SvGrid from intercepting the pointer event and starting its own cell selection.

There's also the question of what happens after the drag ends—some browsers fire a `click` event after `dragend`, so I should stop that too to be safe.

Looking back at the reference demo, it has `enableCellSelection={false}`, which is why it doesn't have this problem. But our BOM demo needs cell selection enabled for inline editing, so I can't just disable it. data.reasoningText:
So there's no `enableSorting: false` per column. The doc says: "Not yet first-class — there is no `enableSorting: false` field. To prevent a column from being sortable, do not register `rowSortingFeature`, or wrap the header in a custom component that swallows the click."

But the user wants to eliminate the sort/hover menu for the handle column only. The existing BOM data does use `rowSortingFeature` because the current code has:


The drag functionality we just added actually replaces sorting anyway. Since BOM is tree-based rather than a flat sortable list, sorting doesn't really apply here. I'm thinking we should probably just remove `rowSortingFeature` entirely from the BOM implementation.

Looking at what the user actually asked for - they want the handle column wider to accommodate the drag handle, and they want to remove those sort options that appear on hover in the header. So I need to widen the first column from 40px to around 56-60px, and disable the sorting UI specifically for that column header.

The issue is there's no per-column way to disable sorting in this setup. Since the BOM is a tree structure that doesn't need sorting anyway, the cleanest approach is to just remove `rowSortingFeature` from the features entirely - that'll eliminate the sort indicators from all headers. The type system should handle this fine since the columns don't actually depend on any sorting-specific properties.

I should also set the handle column width to 56px while I'm at it.

The custom header approach would preserve sorting for other columns while keeping the handle column unaffected. So the real issue is suppressing those hover options on the handle column's header specifically. Looking at the existing demos, the handle column already has an empty header string, so the header text itself is blank. The sorting feature is what's triggering those interactive options to appear on hover. Since there's no way to disable sorting per-column, I'll remove `rowSortingFeature` entirely from the table configuration—it makes more sense for a BOM anyway since sorting individual rows doesn't have semantic meaning. This means updating the imports, removing it from the features array, and adjusting the column definition type accordingly. I should also widen the handle column while I'm at it.

data.content: No hay prop para deshabilitarlo por columna — el botón siempre se renderiza y se muestra vía CSS en hover. Pero el `<th>` tiene `data-svgrid-header-col="{columnId}"`, así que puedo ocultarlo con CSS puntual para la columna `handle`:

toolRequests.name: replace_string_in_file

data.content: El `<th>` del handle column tiene `data-svgrid-header-col="handle"` (lo vi en el source), así que `:global(th[data-svgrid-header-col="handle"] .sv-grid-col-menu-btn) { display: none }` apunta exactamente al botón hamburguesa de esa columna sin afectar las demás.
